nodes.brief: Jovian constitutional rule: a consciousness in the Construct must also have a physical body connected to it.
nodes.master_summary: The structural safeguard against pure-Construct existence. Connecting body to mind kept Jovians tethered to physical reality and locked their brains into the Construct's compute substrate. Violations of this law underwrite most of the canonical Jove failure modes that follow.

nodes.name: The Deathless
nodes.brief: Mysterious figure first surfacing YC110 era. Operates the Deathless Network, distributes intel and resources across pirate factions, currently seen pulling Archbine away from Mordu's Legion / Mu and into his own circle.
nodes.master_summary: The current cycle's antagonist-of-record. Modern Deathless arc converges multiple long-running threads: stolen capsuleer-grade resources, unknown connection to Jove deep history (the name + immortality framing reads as Construct-derived), Pochven operations, Archbine defection. Counter-target of Intaki Bank / Upwell counter-insurgency.

nodes.name: Deathless Network
nodes.brief: Distribution arm of the Deathless. Seeds intel and resources to pirate factions; documented role in distributing capsuleer-grade resources stolen by Esri-class operations to other pirate groups.
nodes.master_summary: The Deathless Network is the operational layer between the Deathless himself and the pirate ecosystem. Functions as a black-market intel exchange — same shape as the Antagonists-chronicle Sentona/Lacante/Ishukone data trade, but with the Deathless rather than Ishukone as the terminal recipient. Where Intaki Bank's counter-insurgency is aimed.

nodes.name: Thera
nodes.brief: Wormhole-space (Anoikis) system. Sisters of EVE's secret research base, revealed publicly during the Drifter-era reveal. Staging point for SoE / Drifter / Triglavian research.
nodes.master_summary: The Sisters of EVE's deep-research base, hidden in Anoikis until externally discovered. Sentona Sarpati's organization runs its non-humanitarian work from here. Structurally adjacent to every Drifter / Sleeper / hyperspace-network thread — the SoE-AIR-EDENCOM research pipeline runs through this site. Silphy's SoE training era likely intersected the Thera operation, though the published chronicles don't place her there directly.

nodes.name: Muryia Mordu
nodes.brief: Founder of Mordu's Legion. Implicit referent in the Antagonists chronicle's 'mercenaries' line — the unnamed commander Silphy works around in her Ishukone data trade.
nodes.master_summary: Mordu's Legion's founder. Independent mercenary commander operating outside the four-empire structure. Antagonists-chronicle subtext: the 'mercenaries' Silphy negotiates around when offering her Zephyr-telemetry data to Ishukone — Mens won't take the deal directly while Mordu's people are in the way. Mordu's Legion's separate relationship to Ishukone via the Templar One clone-soldier program ties him structurally to the Mens-Reppola axis.

nodes.name: Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Original Caldari homeworld in the Luminaire system. Orbitally bombarded by Gallente during the Caldari Rebellion; the surviving exiles founded New Caldari Prime. Recovered by Tibus Heth's Provost coup; later re-administered under Federation oversight.
nodes.master_summary: The lost homeworld at the heart of the Caldari-Gallente schism. Cyclically contested across centuries — bombarded by Gallente in the Rebellion, abandoned by surviving Caldari, reclaimed by Heth in the Empyrean Age, then re-administered diplomatically. Caldari Prime is the symbolic counterweight to New Caldari Prime: one is the lost mother, the other the chosen successor. Every Caldari–Gallente political beat references this duality. Heth's coup gave him the political capital to absorb Ytiri and reshape mega-corp alignment; without Caldari Prime there is no Provost era and no Mountain-vs-Forest realignment.

nodes.name: Tibus Heth
nodes.brief: Caldari populist Provost. Rose from Kaalakiota blue-collar leadership to lead the coup that reclaimed Caldari Prime, then forged the Provost alliance that absorbed Ytiri and remade Caldari mega-corp politics for ~5 years.
nodes.master_summary: The most disruptive Caldari political figure of the modern era. Heth's reclamation of Caldari Prime turned populist anger into a cross-corp alliance — the Provost — that swept aside the post-rebellion mega-corp consensus. Under Heth, Ytiri was absorbed into the alliance (taking Star Seed onto the Ishukone-axis side), Sukuuvestaa pushed back, and the State briefly operated as a single political bloc. His fall ~YC115 left a power vacuum that the Mountain + Ocean factions filled, completing the Lai-Dai-vs-Ishukone realignment Ash now diagrams.

nodes.name: Kaalakiota Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in heavy industry and weapons manufacturing. Tibus Heth's home corporation; the Provost alliance's industrial spine.
nodes.master_summary: One of the eight Caldari mega-corps. Kaalakiota's manufacturing base was the political launching pad for the Provost alliance — Heth used the corp's blue-collar workforce as a populist constituency. Post-Heth, Kaalakiota settled into the Mountain-faction realignment alongside Ishukone. Its weapons output makes it the State's primary military-industrial supplier, structurally similar to Lai Dai's commercial position but with a more austere internal culture.

nodes.name: Sukuuvestaa Corporation
nodes.brief: Largest Caldari mega-corp by revenue. Insurance, heavy industry, and agricultural conglomerate. Politically conservative; Heth-era resistor of Provost absorption.
nodes.master_summary: The Caldari State's economic backbone. Sukuuvestaa runs the largest civilian workforce of any Caldari corporation, which gives it political weight without needing Provost-style coalitions. Resisted Heth's absorption attempts; emerged from the Provost era institutionally intact, which made it the natural anchor of the post-Heth Mountain faction. Insurance arm is the State's structural memory — Sukuuvestaa records every claim made against every other mega-corp's operations.

nodes.name: Hyasyoda Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in agriculture, biotech, and consumer goods. Forest-faction-aligned per Ash's whiteboard.
nodes.master_summary: The State's agricultural and biotech anchor. Less politically visible than Ishukone or Lai Dai, but holds the food-supply leverage that becomes critical when border restrictions tighten — which is exactly what happened during the Jita-gate post-incident curfews. Forest-faction-aligned, making Hyasyoda a Lai Dai-axis sister-corp on Ash's structural read of Caldari politics.

nodes.name: Wiyrkomi Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in shipbuilding and naval engineering. Builds the Wyvern supercarrier and other State flagships.
nodes.master_summary: The Caldari Navy's primary capital-ship builder. Wiyrkomi's Wyvern-class line is the structural anchor of the State's super-fleet doctrine; loss of a Wiyrkomi yard is a strategic event. Mountain-faction-aligned and tightly coordinated with Kaalakiota's munitions output — the two together form the Mountain/Ocean military-industrial cluster that the Forest faction (Lai Dai-led commercial trade) doesn't have a direct counter for.

nodes.name: Tash-Murkon (region)
nodes.brief: Amarr region under House Tash-Murkon's stewardship. Youngest of the five royal-house regions. Adjacent to the Khanid Kingdom and Aridia.
nodes.master_summary: The most progressive Amarr royal region — House Tash-Murkon is the only Udorian-bloodline royal house and is structurally less conservative on slave reform than Sarum, Kor-Azor, Kador, or Ardishapur. The region's geography puts it adjacent to Khanid (the defected kingdom) and Aridia (the lawless border), which historically positions Tash-Murkon as the empire's softest political edge. Mordu's Legion runs significant operations here.

nodes.name: Sebiestor Tribe
nodes.brief: One of the seven Mimitar tribes. Engineering-focused; the Republic's industrial backbone. The Sebiestor Tribe corporation is the political face within Republic governance.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's engineering tribe — produces the bulk of Republic naval shipwrights, capsule technologists, and infrastructure engineers. Less mystically-inclined than the Vherokior, less martial than the Brutor or Krusual; the structural counterweight inside the Tribal Council that keeps the Republic's industrial pipeline running. Most likely candidate for a Mimitar-side Mil-Campaign zone after Starkmanir, given its visibility in the Republic's modern self-image.

nodes.name: Angel Cartel
nodes.brief: Major pirate faction operating from Curse, Great Wildlands, and Stain-adjacent space. Loose criminal alliance with deep ties to Mordu's Legion's founding personnel.
nodes.master_summary: Southern New Eden's enduring pirate organization. Angel Cartel financing has been documented in multiple Caldari–Gallente political destabilization events; the four-empire posture toward them is performatively hostile, structurally tolerant. Mordu's Legion's founding membership came from Angel Cartel deserters — the relationship between the two is more genealogical than antagonistic. Skarkon's defection to Cartel administration ~YC110 is the canonical demonstration of how an empire-region falls when the Republic stops servicing it.

nodes.name: Intaki
nodes.brief: Federation-member people; one of the founding Gallente bloodlines. Distinct from the Intaki Syndicate (the dissident exile group that retained the name in nullsec). Religious tradition: Ida faith with reincarnation belief.
nodes.master_summary: The original Intaki bloodline — peaceful, philosophical, religiously distinct via the Ida faith's reincarnation cosmology. Founding members of the Federation, but always retained cultural autonomy that produced both the Caldari-sympathetic exile (the Syndicate) and the Lost-Adama religious arc (Eloa Enaro / Negui Erlon, YC120). The Federation's seizure of Intaki in YC124 — what triggered the Syndicate–Caldari constellation deal — was an act against this people, not against the Syndicate. Moreau's amnesty offer post-conquest is structurally an attempt to reach the Intaki proper, not the Syndicate fugitives.

nodes.name: Nefantar Tribe
nodes.brief: The Mimitar tribe that defected to the Amarr during the Day of Darkness, renamed 'Ammatar' under Damyas III's honorific. Restored to Republic membership in YC115 under Alika Valkanir; many descendants still administer the Ammatar Mandate.
nodes.master_summary: The Nefantar are the Mimitar tribe whose Day-of-Darkness collaboration produced the Ammatar Mandate. The Imperian-Age reveal showed they had been covertly hiding Starkmanir survivors during the centuries of Amarr rule — partial redemption that recategorized them from outright traitors to long-game protectors. Restored as the seventh Mimitar tribe in YC115. The split between 'Nefantar' (tribal identity, Republic-restored) and 'Ammatar' (mandate-administering descendants still loyal to the Empire) is the structural backbone of any future Mil-Campaign #2 zone.

nodes.name: Brutor Tribe
nodes.brief: Mimitar warrior tribe. Most martially-inclined of the seven; the Republic's primary infantry / capsuleer-pilot recruitment pool. Brutor Chief Ukumi Pol named the Amarr explicitly during the Jita-gate crisis.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's warrior tribe — produces the bulk of Republic naval officers, ground-force infantry, and capsuleer pilots. Politically the most hawkish tribal voice. Brutor Chief Ukumi Pol's YC128 statement pointing fingers at the Amarr is consistent with Brutor's longstanding posture: the tribe has never bought into the appeasement cycles that Krusual or Sebiestor leadership periodically pursue.

nodes.name: Krusual Tribe
nodes.brief: Mimitar mountain-people tribe. Politically conservative, traditional, and the source of the 'Krusual' (the unbroken-Mimitar) cultural identity that resisted full Amarr enslavement.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's mountain tribe — geographically isolated during the Amarr era, which preserved more of the pre-Empire culture than any other Republic tribe retains. Politically conservative and resistant to Galante-style democratic reform. The Krusoral identity (which Ash highlights) is the Krusual self-conception: the unbent, unbroken, never-fully-enslaved Mimitar lineage.

nodes.name: Black Rise
nodes.brief: Caldari frontier nullsec region adjacent to The Forge and Lonetrek. Lai Dai administrative target during the Black Rise development push (~YC110). Faction-warfare zone with Gallente.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari's eastern frontier region. Lai Dai's announced 'Black Rise development' was a major mid-2010s commercial-expansion play that put the corp's resources into the borderland. Its FW status against Gallente makes it the eastern counterpart to Placid (the western FW border) — together they bracket the Caldari–Gallente conflict zone that Warpath Protocol Failure exposed.

nodes.name: Molden Heath
nodes.brief: Mimitar frontier region. Adjacent to Heimatar and Great Wildlands. Krusual / Brutor military operations zone; structurally the Republic's eastern flank.
nodes.master_summary: Republic frontier region. Molden Heath is where the Republic's traditional military architecture (Brutor / Krusual deployments) projects power outward. Adjacent to Great Wildlands, which Thukker semi-autonomously administers, making the Molden Heath ↔ Great Wildlands border the Republic's tribal-to-tribal handoff. Ash flags Molden Heath as a possible Mimitar-side Mil-Campaign zone if the Ammatar Mandate prediction plays out.

nodes.name: Great Wildlands
nodes.brief: NPC-nullsec region. Thukker tribal territory; Mordu's Legion operations zone. Closest Mimitar analogue to the Intaki Syndicate's NPC-nullsec status in Syndicate region.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar tribal frontier at NPC scale. Thukker administers; Mordu's Legion operates from. Structurally the tribe's parallel to Intaki Syndicate's relationship with the Federation — semi-autonomous, NPC-controlled, semi-deniable. Prismatic / fractured-site activity has been heavy here during the Catalyst era. Drifter-tech research that the Republic-proper doesn't want to claim happens through Great Wildlands intermediaries.

nodes.name: Caille
nodes.brief: Capital city of the Gallente Federation, located on Gallente Prime. Seat of the Federation Senate, Presidential office, and Yulai-aligned diplomatic infrastructure.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Prime's capital city — political center of the Federation. Senatorial nominations, presidential inaugurations, and the Yulai diplomatic apparatus all anchor here. Moreau's YC128 inauguration was held in Caille under the Jita-gate cloud. Symbolic counterweight to New Caldari Prime (Caldari political center) and Amarr (Empire center) — the three-empire political-capital geography is one of CCP's clearest storytelling anchors.

nodes.name: Caldari Business Tribunal
nodes.brief: Caldari judicial / regulatory body. Investigated the financial corruption operating out of Jita that — per Saraki's CEP statement — turned out to be Drifter-hyperspace-network manipulation, not conventional fraud.
nodes.master_summary: The CBT is the Caldari State's mega-corp regulator. Long-running investigation of Jita financial corruption was the diplomatic cover under which Saraki revealed (during the post-Jita-gate CEP statement) that the 'corruption' was actually weaponized hyperspace-network exploitation. CBT operates in tension with the House of Records: where the latter tracks raw transaction history, the CBT decides what's legal. Forest-faction-aligned per the structural pattern (Lai-Dai-administered commerce gets the regulatory seat).

nodes.name: Sansha's Nation
nodes.brief: Cybernetic-pirate empire founded by Sansha Kuvakei. True-slave neural-conditioning technology. Periodic incursion campaigns against high-sec systems; structural counterpart to the Drifter / Triglavian incursion patterns.
nodes.master_summary: The fourth major canonical pirate empire — Sansha's signature is the True-Slave neural conditioning, an extreme of the Vitoxin / mind-control technology spectrum. Periodic incursions historically targeted both high-sec and faction territory; many of CCP's earlier expansion arcs (incursion mechanic, response protocols) were shaped around Sansha's behavior pattern. Genealogically related to the Sarpati line through Sansha Kuvakei's family — one of the few canonical bridges between the southern pirate factions and the Sarpati black-market network.

nodes.name: Heideran VII
nodes.brief: Amarr Emperor who preceded Doriam Kor-Azor. Long-reigning, conservative; presided over the Empire during the early modern era of capsuleer activity.
nodes.master_summary: The pre-modern Amarr throne anchor. Heideran's death triggered the championship that produced Doriam Kor-Azor — the succession that Karsoth later subverted. Heideran's long reign is the structural reason the Empire's political institutions are as conservative as they are: his persistence prevented the kind of generational turnover the other empires got, leaving the Amarr political system unusually rigid by the time the Empyrean Age destabilization hit.

nodes.name: Doriam Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: Doriam II. Amarr Emperor between Heideran VII and Jamyl I. Reformist; assassinated under circumstances that put Karsoth into the Provost Marshal seat and effectively ran the Empire from there.
nodes.master_summary: Doriam Kor-Azor was the brief reformist throne between Heideran's long conservatism and Jamyl's superweapon-defined reign. His assassination is one of the most consequential political events of the modern era — it left Karsoth (a Provost Marshal) in operational control of the Empire while the Royal Heirs were unable to consolidate succession. The Karsoth interregnum is when most of the structural Amarr-vs-Republic instability that fed into the Imperian Age was set up.

nodes.name: Aritcio Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: House Kor-Azor heir, son of Doriam II. Implicated in the corrupt-Provost Marshal era; House Kor-Azor's reputation took a long-term hit through this line.
nodes.master_summary: The Kor-Azor heir whose involvement in Provost Marshal politics tarnished the house's standing through the post-Doriam interregnum. Structurally important as the named successor whose corruption became the explicit reason House Kor-Azor lost political weight relative to Sarum and Tash-Murkon during Jamyl's rise. Modern Amarr royal politics still references the Kor-Azor diminishment as a cautionary precedent.

nodes.name: Karsoth
nodes.brief: Provost Marshal who took de facto control of the Amarr Empire after Doriam Kor-Azor's assassination. Opposed Royal Heir succession until Jamyl Sarum's emergence forced the championship.
nodes.master_summary: The Karsoth interregnum is the modern Amarr's structural disaster — a Provost Marshal running the Empire's day-to-day while the throne sat vacant. His refusal to concede succession to the Royal Heirs is the proximate cause of much of the Empire's mid-modern instability and explains why Jamyl I's emergence was framed as restoration rather than coronation. Karsoth's tenure is also the structural ancestor of the kind of office-holder-vs-throne tension that any modern Amarr political beat references.

nodes.name: House Sarum
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. The most martial / military-traditionalist of the five; produced Jamyl Sarum (Empress Jamyl I).
nodes.master_summary: House Sarum's institutional identity is military sufficiency. Their territorial seat is centered on Sarum Prime (destroyed by Jamyl's superweapon during the Elder Fleet attack — an in-house event with massive symbolic weight). Sarum's combat-doctrine focus made it the natural counterweight to Karsoth's bureaucratic interregnum, and Jamyl's emergence from this house reset the Empire's institutional center of gravity for a generation.

nodes.name: House Tash-Murkon
nodes.brief: The youngest of the five Amarr royal houses, and the only one of Udorian rather than Amarr bloodline. Stewards the Tash-Murkon Region (territorial seat). Politically the empire's softest edge.
nodes.master_summary: House Tash-Murkon's distinctness is structural — Udorian rather than Amarr bloodline means it operates inside the Amarr political system without quite being of it. The youngest house, the most progressive on slave reform, and the natural diplomatic interlocutor when Empire–Federation or Empire–Republic talks need a non-confrontational Amarr counterparty. Tash-Murkon's region is also the Empire's southern frontier with Khanid (defected) and Aridia (lawless), giving the house operational reasons to maintain pragmatic borders.

nodes.name: House Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. Doriam Kor-Azor's house. Reformist tradition tarnished by Aritcio's Provost-era corruption.
nodes.master_summary: House Kor-Azor's institutional identity is reformist: Doriam II's brief reign was the modern Empire's last significant attempt at reform-from-the-throne. The house's reputation took a long-term hit when Aritcio's involvement in the Karsoth-era corrupt Provost Marshal apparatus tarnished the lineage. Kor-Azor's reduced political weight relative to Sarum and Tash-Murkon during Jamyl's rise is the structural memory of that failure — and the cautionary precedent every future Amarr reformist program references.

nodes.name: House Kador
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. The most conservative slaver-traditionalist of the five. Hardline on the religious-political axis.
nodes.master_summary: House Kador anchors the conservative end of Amarr royal politics — most committed to traditional slaver economics, most resistant to the kind of incremental reform Tash-Murkon and Kor-Azor periodically push. When the Empire needs an institutional voice for 'the old order is non-negotiable', Kador provides it. Strategically aligned with Ardishapur on most religious-political questions; structurally opposed to Tash-Murkon's pragmatic accommodation strategies.

nodes.name: House Ardishapur
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. Slaver-conservative; the house responsible for the Starkmanir genocide via Archon Ardishapur's son Idonis. Damyas III bestowed the Ammatar honorific on the Nefantar.
nodes.master_summary: House Ardishapur carries the historical weight of the Starkmanir genocide — Archon Ardishapur's hardline replacement of the liberal Arzad Hamri provoked the uprising, and Idonis's orbital bombardment annihilated Starkmanir Prime. The Imperian Age reveal that the Ammatar had been hiding Starkmanir survivors throughout was a direct rebuke to Ardishapur's centuries-long claim that the genocide was complete. House Ardishapur's modern political posture is aligned with Kador on conservative slaver economics; its reputational baggage limits its diplomatic reach.

nodes.name: Sansha Kuvakei
nodes.brief: Founder of Sansha's Nation. Megalomaniac who pursued forced cybernetic enslavement under the banner of post-human evolution. Killed in YC36; later resurrected as a True Slave-Master.
nodes.master_summary: Founder of Sansha's Nation. Megalomaniac who pursued forced cybernetic enslavement under the banner of post-human evolution. Killed in YC36, then later resurrected as a True Slave-Master and continues to coordinate Sansha incursions across New Eden. The structural prototype for every later 'transcend humanity through forced merger' antagonist — including the Deathless framing.

nodes.name: Malkalen Disaster
nodes.brief: YC110 Caldari–Federation summit aboard Ishukone Headquarters station at Malkalen. Alexander Noir's Nyx struck the station with safety protocols disabled, killing Otro Gariushi and triggering the State's invasion of Caldari Prime in YC112.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Caldari–Federation summit aboard Ishukone Headquarters station at Malkalen. Alexander Noir's Nyx struck the station with safety protocols disabled, killing Otro Gariushi and tens of thousands. The 'broker' plot targeted Otro to stop Ishukone's Insorum development. The disaster became Tibus Heth's pretext to militarize the State and triggered the YC112 Caldari invasion of Caldari Prime — the largest interstate war of the modern era.

nodes.name: Triglavian Collective
nodes.brief: Abyssal humanoid civilization that emerged from Abyssal Deadspace. Conquered 27 systems via the Invasion arc to form Pochven. Tied to abyssal-filament tech and the Drifter conflict.
nodes.master_summary: Abyssal humanoid civilization that emerged from Abyssal Deadspace via the Drifter Crisis upheavals. Conquered 27 systems through the Invasion arc to form Pochven. Tied to abyssal-filament technology and a multi-front conflict with the Drifters. Their proxis-class doctrine sits adjacent to Construct-derived tech without using it the same way — making them a third pole next to Drifter and capsuleer civilizations.

nodes.name: Pochven
nodes.brief: Triglavian-controlled region formed from the 27 Invasion-arc conquered systems. Bordered region between Triglavian space and standard New Eden. Site of recent Deathless / Archbine activity.
nodes.master_summary: Triglavian-controlled region formed from the 27 Invasion-arc conquered systems. Bordered space between Triglavian abyssal territory and standard New Eden. Recent Deathless / Archbine activity has used Pochven as a staging zone, and the Zarzakh-to-Pochven corridor is one of the few non-empire transit routes the Deathless Network exploits.

nodes.name: Zarzakh
nodes.brief: The Deathless's home base. Hidden system with EVE-gate-adjacent properties. Coordination center for the Deathless Network.
nodes.master_summary: The Deathless's home base. A hidden system with EVE-gate-adjacent properties revealed during the Equinox-era Deathless arc. The Deathless Network's coordination hub — pirate factions report to Zarzakh, Archbine defectors transit through it, and the Shikari operations are run from it. Sits structurally where Pochven meets the Eve Gate periphery.

nodes.name: Provists (Caldari Provist regime)
nodes.brief: Tibus Heth's authoritarian faction that seized control of the Caldari State after the Malkalen Disaster. Brutal labor / militaristic ideology; rebranded the State along Mountain-faction lines.
nodes.master_summary: Tibus Heth's authoritarian faction that seized de-facto control of the Caldari State after the Malkalen Disaster (YC110). Built around Kaalakiota labor militancy and Mountain-faction ideology, the Provists ran the State through the YC112 invasion of Caldari Prime, the YC115 Caldari–Federation conflict resolution, and into the Empyrean Age. Their downfall — the rise of Akimaka Saraki and the post-Heth consolidation — is the proximate cause of the current Lai-Dai-vs-Ishukone axis.

nodes.name: Caldari Invasion of Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Massive Caldari State military operation that retook Caldari Prime from Federation control. Heth's signature war, justified as restitution for Malkalen.
nodes.master_summary: The YC112 Caldari State invasion of Caldari Prime — the retaking of the homeworld lost during the Caldari Rebellion. Tibus Heth justified the operation as restitution for the Malkalen Disaster (YC110). Mordu's Legion participated under contract, then later announced departure. The invasion locked in Heth's Provist regime and established the modern Caldari claim on Caldari Prime that the YC128 conflict is once again contesting.

nodes.name: Alexander Noir
nodes.brief: Capsuleer pilot of the Nyx mothership that struck Ishukone Headquarters at Malkalen. Officially named the perpetrator; broker plot evidence suggests he was framed and the safety protocols were disabled by other parties.
nodes.master_summary: Capsuleer pilot of the Nyx mothership that struck Ishukone Headquarters at Malkalen, killing Otro Gariushi and tens of thousands. Officially named the perpetrator of the Malkalen Disaster, but the broker plot evidence — station and ship safety protocols disabled at the same time — suggests he was set up to take the blame for the Insorum-targeted assassination. The structural ambiguity around Noir is what makes Malkalen 'Disaster' canonically rather than 'Attack.'

nodes.name: Aldrith Shutaq
nodes.brief: Khanid noble and Provost-Marshal-era figure mentioned in the Heth coverage. Royal Khanid Navy lineage.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid noble in the Provost-Marshal-era news cycle. Royal Khanid Navy lineage. Frequently surfaces as a Khanid voice in the Pure Faith Harvest / Lost Adama era and the post-Allar-Chakaide Khanid succession.

nodes.name: Caldari Navy
nodes.brief: Caldari State military fleet. Backbone of the YC112 Caldari Prime invasion and the current Caldari conquest of Syndicate.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari State unified military fleet. Backbone of every State military operation since the Empyrean Age — the YC112 Caldari Prime invasion, the YC115 capitulation theater, the Triglavian Invasion response, and the current YC128 Syndicate conquest. Operationally controlled by the Caldari Executive Panel through the Provost / now Saraki authority chain.

nodes.name: Federation Navy
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation military fleet. Conducted the YC124 invasion of Intaki + adjacent constellations that triggered the Uprising arc.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation military fleet. Conducted the YC124 invasion of Intaki + adjacent constellations that triggered the Uprising arc and the constellation deal with the Intaki Syndicate that the YC128 conquest now invalidates. Currently under President Moreau's authority — and the hawkish Senate factions worry her Autonomist-Party origins make her unable to direct it firmly.

nodes.name: Amarr Imperial Navy
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire military fleet. Operates across all five Royal Houses' territories under the Empress's direct authority.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire military fleet. Operates across all five Royal Houses' territories under the Empress's direct authority. Currently engaged in operations against Drifters in Khanid space and against capsuleer disruption of slave-shipment lanes — and Catiz I's territorial reorganization restructured their command chain.

nodes.name: Royal Khanid Navy
nodes.brief: Khanid Kingdom's autonomous military fleet, separate from the Imperial Navy. Repulsed the Pure Faith Harvest raid YC120.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Kingdom's autonomous military fleet, separate from the Imperial Navy. Carries the Empire's military burden in the southern frontier — repulsed the Pure Faith Harvest raid YC120, conducted the brutal Kahah pacification under Allar Chakaide, and engages Blood Raider incursions on a sustained basis. The 19th Royal Uolans is one of its elite units.

nodes.name: Capitulation at Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Theater that ended the Caldari–Federation conflict at Caldari Prime. Concluded the Heth-era invasion cycle.
nodes.master_summary: The YC115 capitulation theater that ended the Caldari–Federation conflict at Caldari Prime, concluding the Heth-era invasion cycle that began with the YC112 retake. The terms preserved Caldari sovereignty over Caldari Prime — the settlement the YC128 Syndicate conquest is structurally challenging.

nodes.name: Minmatar Elder War
nodes.brief: YC110 reappearance of the Minmatar Elder Fleet from Anoikis. Triggered Republic invasions of the Ammatar Mandate and Amarr Empire.
nodes.master_summary: The YC110 reappearance of the Minmatar Elder Fleet from Anoikis after centuries of exile. Triggered Republic invasions of the Ammatar Mandate and Amarr Empire, the Empress Jamyl I crisis, and the Sansha capital fleet incident in Jove space. The Elder War is the structural mirror of Heth's YC112 Caldari Prime invasion — both are 'reclamation' wars that reset the four-empire balance.

nodes.name: Seyllin Disaster
nodes.brief: YC111 catastrophic plasma-fire event at Seyllin I that killed everyone on the planet. Triggered the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening and the discovery of Anoikis.
nodes.master_summary: YC111 catastrophic plasma-fire event at Seyllin I that killed everyone on the planet. The Sisters of EVE 'Wormhole Colony Lost' announcement followed. The Seyllin event triggered the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening, the discovery of Anoikis, the Sleeper civilization remnants, and ultimately the Drifter emergence cycle. Every wormhole-tech thread in the warpath board traces back to Seyllin.

nodes.name: Caroline's Star
nodes.brief: YC117 cosmic anomaly — visible new bright object that appeared overnight. Tied to the Drifter emergence and Eve Gate stress events.
nodes.master_summary: YC117 cosmic anomaly — a bright new object that appeared overnight, visible across all four empires simultaneously. The Sisters of EVE issued a statement tying it to the Drifter emergence and Eve Gate stress events. Caroline's Star is structurally the visible signature of the Drifter Hyperspace Network reaching New Eden's normal space — the moment the Drifter conflict moved from Anoikis backstory to current threat.

nodes.name: True Slaves
nodes.brief: Sansha cybernetic forced-merger drones produced from captured slaves. Sansha Kuvakei's signature technology and the True Power Project's product.
nodes.master_summary: Sansha cybernetic forced-merger drones produced from captured slaves via the True Power Project. The pharmacological-and-implant complement to Vitoxin — Sansha's tech weaponizes merger; Amarr's tech weaponizes dependence; Mordu/Ishukone's clone-soldier tech sits structurally between them. Sansha Kuvakei's signature technology and the canonical prototype for every later 'forced post-human' framing including the Deathless arc.

nodes.name: Sleeper Civilization
nodes.brief: Lost Jovian-descended civilization in Anoikis wormhole space. Awakened during the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening; structurally tied to the Construct and the Drifter emergence.
nodes.master_summary: Lost Jovian-descended civilization in Anoikis wormhole space — Jove consciousnesses that committed fully to the Construct and never came out. Awakened during the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening (post-Seyllin). The Sleeper-to-Drifter transition is one of the Drifter Crisis's central canonical mysteries; AIR Labs and SoE both research the boundary.

nodes.name: State Protectorate
nodes.brief: Caldari Faction Warfare militia. Operates the State's frontline against Federation forces in the FW war zone.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari Faction Warfare militia under Caldari Navy authority. The State's volunteer / capsuleer-pilot frontline against the Federation in the Black Rise / Placid / Verge Vendor war zone. The YC124 Federation seizure of Intaki + adjacent constellations that triggered the Uprising arc was an FW-zone consolidation against the Protectorate.

nodes.name: Federal Defense Union
nodes.brief: Gallente Faction Warfare militia. Federation's frontline in the FW war zone.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation Faction Warfare militia. The Federation's volunteer / capsuleer-pilot frontline against the State in the FW war zone. Operationally the FDU executed the YC124 Intaki seizure that the Republic and SoE later condemned. Operates under Federation Navy authority.

nodes.name: 24th Imperial Crusade
nodes.brief: Amarr Faction Warfare militia. The Empire's frontline against the Republic in the Amarr–Minmatar war zone.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Faction Warfare militia. The Empire's volunteer / capsuleer-pilot frontline against the Minmatar Republic in the Bleak Lands / Devoid / Heimatar war zone. Penal brigades drawn from the Ammatar Mandate were sent to support the Crusade — one of the canonical signals of Ammatar's Amarr-loyalist alignment.

nodes.name: Tribal Liberation Force
nodes.brief: Minmatar Faction Warfare militia. The Republic's frontline against the Amarr in the FW war zone.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar Republic Faction Warfare militia. The Republic's volunteer / capsuleer-pilot frontline against the Empire. The 'Stripes of Fury' campaign cycle is its highest-honor designation. Operates under the Tribal Council's authority.

nodes.name: Omir Sarikusa
nodes.brief: Founder and current leader of the Guristas. Former Caldari Navy admiral who defected with his entire fleet.
nodes.master_summary: Founder and current leader of the Guristas. Former Caldari Navy admiral who defected with his entire fleet, taking the original Guristas pirate organization with him. Operates from Venal region. The Guristas–Mordu's Legion–Angel Cartel cross-coverage in the Caldari conflict cycle indicates an active pirate-faction alignment that capsuleer coverage rarely surfaces.

nodes.name: Capsuleers
nodes.brief: The immortal pilot caste. Subcranial-implant-and-cloning tech grants effective immortality. A canonical political force across all four empires.
nodes.master_summary: The immortal pilot caste — humans who undergo subcranial-implant-and-cloning tech to gain effective immortality and operate the four empires' most advanced spacecraft. Recognized as a sovereign-equivalent political force across the four empires. Key actor in Faction Warfare, Drifter conflict, and the YC128 cycle (capsuleer pilots elected Soraya Roden / Alix Moreau influence cycle). The Templar program is the Caldari attempt to replicate capsuleer-tier capability without the Federation's clone-tech monopoly.

nodes.name: Apocrypha-era Wormhole Opening
nodes.brief: YC111 system-wide wormhole opening triggered by Seyllin. Exposed Anoikis to capsuleer access and awoke Sleeper-civilization remnants.
nodes.master_summary: The YC111 system-wide wormhole opening triggered by the Seyllin Disaster. Exposed Anoikis (wormhole space) to capsuleer access for the first time, awoke Sleeper-civilization remnants, and started the chain of Drifter exposure that culminates in the YC125 Drifter Crisis. The Zephyr distribution YC111 was structurally a Syndicate response to the opening.

nodes.name: Clone Soldier / Templar Technology
nodes.brief: Subcranial-implant-and-cloning tech adapted from capsuleer hardware for ground-troop use. Co-developed by Ishukone and Mordu's Legion. Templar protagonist of the Templar One book.
nodes.master_summary: Subcranial-implant-and-cloning tech adapted from capsuleer hardware for ground-troop use. Co-developed by Ishukone and Mordu's Legion under Otro Gariushi's mandate; survived his assassination because Mens Reppola inherited the program. The Templar protagonist of Tony Gonzales's Templar One is the canonical instance. The State's strategic answer to the Federation–CONCORD capsuleer-tech monopoly. Empress Jamyl I's offer to welcome clone soldiers into the Empire was a direct play for Templar-tech access.

nodes.name: EVE Gate
nodes.brief: The collapsed wormhole connecting New Eden to humanity's home cluster. Located in New Eden system. Iconic anomaly; Sisters of EVE research focus.
nodes.master_summary: The collapsed wormhole connecting New Eden to humanity's home cluster. Located in New Eden system. The original passage through which the four-empire ancestors arrived; collapsed catastrophically in pre-history (the eve_gate_collapse event), stranding the colonists. Sisters of EVE research focus; structurally adjacent to the Drifter network and Caroline's Star phenomena. Zarzakh sits in EVE-Gate-adjacent space.

nodes.name: Amarr Reclaiming
nodes.brief: Pre-history Amarr conquest cycle. The Amarr Empire's expansion via subjugation, including the conquest of the Minmatar.
nodes.master_summary: Pre-history Amarr conquest cycle — the religious-imperial expansion that built the Amarr Empire via the subjugation of all neighboring peoples. The Minmatar conquest is its climactic chapter; the Khanid secession is its first major failure; the Battle of Vak'Atioth (when the Jove repulsed Amarr expansion) was its eventual ceiling. Every Amarr-driven storyline traces back to Reclaiming theology.

nodes.name: Battle of Vak'Atioth
nodes.brief: Battle in which the Jove Empire repulsed Amarr expansion, halting the Reclaiming and establishing the modern Jove-Amarr cold peace.
nodes.master_summary: Battle in which the Jove Empire annihilated an Amarr Reclaiming fleet and halted the Empire's expansion at the Jove border. Established the modern Jove-Amarr cold peace and locked in the four-empire balance. The structural ceiling on Amarr ambition; every later Amarr crisis (Khanid secession, Minmatar Rebellion, Heideran reform, Catiz reorganization) operates inside the Vak'Atioth-defined frontier.

nodes.name: Minmatar Rebellion (Great Rebellion)
nodes.brief: YC ~80s slave revolt that broke Amarr rule over the Minmatar peoples and founded the modern Republic.
nodes.master_summary: YC ~80s slave revolt that broke Amarr rule over the Minmatar peoples and founded the modern Republic. Triggered by the Day of Darkness backstory and the suppression cycle that followed; concluded with the seven-tribes restoration. The Ammatar Mandate's original split (Amarr-loyalist Minmatar vs Republic-loyalist) dates to this cycle. Every Minmatar storyline traces back to the Rebellion.

nodes.name: Caldari Rebellion
nodes.brief: YC ~80s Caldari secession from the Gallente Federation. Founded the Caldari State and the modern four-empire system.
nodes.master_summary: YC ~80s Caldari secession from the Gallente Federation, fought over the Federation's culturally-Gallente integration policy. The Caldari Prime homeworld was lost during the war; the Caldari-sympathetic Intaki exiles who became the Intaki Syndicate emerged from this period. Founded the modern four-empire system. The YC112 invasion of Caldari Prime was framed as the Rebellion's eventual closure.

nodes.name: Triglavian Invasion
nodes.brief: YC122 Triglavian conquest of 27 systems via the abyssal-filament invasion mechanic. Formed Pochven and reset the inter-empire defensive posture.
nodes.master_summary: YC122 Triglavian conquest of 27 systems via the abyssal-filament invasion mechanic. Formed Pochven, opened the Triglavian-Drifter conflict to capsuleer participation, and exposed the Niarja trade-route severance that broke the Amarr-Caldari high-sec corridor. EDENCOM's formation as an inter-empire defensive coalition came directly from the Invasion response. Structurally a third-power eruption between the four-empire system and Drifter forces.

nodes.name: Yulai system
nodes.brief: CONCORD's headquarters system. Site of the Yulai Accords. Symbolic center of inter-empire governance.
nodes.master_summary: CONCORD's headquarters system. Site of the Yulai Accords (the inter-empire governance treaty). Symbolic center of inter-empire governance — and the YC110 Yulai gate breach is the only prior precedent for the YC128 Warpath Protocol failure. Capsuleer SoCT briefings happen here.

nodes.name: Anoikis (wormhole space)
nodes.brief: The wormhole-space cluster — formerly Sleeper civilization territory. Site of Thera, hyperspace network anomalies, and Drifter staging grounds.
nodes.master_summary: The wormhole-space cluster — formerly Sleeper civilization territory, exposed to capsuleer access by the YC111 Apocrypha-era opening. Site of Thera (SoE deep-research base), the Drifter hyperspace network, the prismatic-sites phenomena, and the Sleeper-civilization remnants. Every wormhole-tech storyline operates in or through Anoikis.

nodes.name: Stain region
nodes.brief: Sansha's Nation home region. Sov-null space; site of True Slave production.
nodes.master_summary: Sansha's Nation home region in southern null-sec. The True Power Project's industrial base — slaves are processed into True Slaves here. The geographical complement of Sansha's incursion campaigns: incursions reach into high-sec from Stain.

nodes.name: Venal region
nodes.brief: Guristas Pirates' home region. Sov-null north of Caldari space.
nodes.master_summary: Guristas Pirates' home region. Sov-null north of Caldari space; the operational base from which Omir Sarikusa's Caldari Navy defectors run their pirate empire. Mordu's Legion and Angel Cartel cross-border ops with Guristas trace through Venal.

nodes.name: Curse region
nodes.brief: Angel Cartel home region. Sov-null south of Minmatar space.
nodes.master_summary: Angel Cartel home region in southern null-sec. Operational base for the Cartel's smuggling and mercenary operations. Mordu's Legion has historic Cartel connections — Mordu's Legion's break from the Cartel is the founding myth of the Legion.

nodes.name: Imperial Court
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire's executive court. Court Chamberlain office (held by Karsoth during the succession crisis era).
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire's executive court. The Court Chamberlain office serves as regent during succession gaps; held by Dochuta Karsoth during the post-Doriam-II era and used to subvert the Sarum succession. The Court is the procedural layer between the Empress and the Five Royal Houses; Catiz I's territorial-reorganization decree was administered through it.

nodes.name: Tribal Council
nodes.brief: Minmatar Republic's governing body. Comprises tribal chiefs of each Minmatar tribe under the Sanmatar's chairmanship.
nodes.master_summary: The Minmatar Republic's governing body. Comprises tribal chiefs of each Minmatar tribe (Brutor, Sebiestor, Krusual, Vherokior, Nefantar, Starkmanir, Thukker) under the Sanmatar's chairmanship. Approved Upwell workforce transfers post-conquest, drives the seven-tribes restoration narrative, and is the operational layer that gives the Republic its coalition pluralism. Maleatu Shakor (Sanmatar) chairs; Karin Midular (Sebiestor Chief) serves as senior diplomatic voice.

nodes.name: Domain region
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire's core region. Contains Amarr (the capital system) and the major imperial throne worlds.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire's core region containing Amarr system, the imperial throne worlds, and the Empress's residence. Capital of Reclaiming-era Amarr space. Heideran VII / Doriam II / Catiz I succession all played out here. Sarum Prime sits in Domain.

nodes.name: Heimatar region
nodes.brief: Minmatar Republic core region. Contains Pator (the Minmatar capital system) and Matar.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar Republic core region. Contains Pator (the capital system) and Matar (the Minmatar homeworld). Center of post-Rebellion tribal restoration; Sanmatar Shakur's seat of government. Salvation Crusaders' Heimatar firefight is the canonical recent event in the region.

nodes.name: Lonetrek region
nodes.brief: Caldari State core region. Contains Jita (the trade hub) and the major State industrial zones.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari State core region. Contains Jita (the trade hub) and the major State industrial zones. Operational center for State Protectorate FW militia operations and the State Workers' Union activity. The Jita-gate incident occurred at Lonetrek's primary trade artery.

nodes.name: Genesis region
nodes.brief: Federation–CONCORD borderland region. Contains Yulai (CONCORD HQ) and the EVE-Gate-adjacent territory.
nodes.master_summary: Federation–CONCORD borderland region. Contains Yulai (CONCORD HQ) and EVE-Gate-adjacent territory. The structural neutral zone between Federation and Empire spheres. Catiz I's territorial reorganization affected the Genesis frontier.

nodes.name: The Bleak Lands
nodes.brief: Faction Warfare war zone — Amarr-controlled but contested by Republic. Location of the Amarr–Minmatar frontline.
nodes.master_summary: Faction Warfare war zone region — Amarr-controlled but contested by Republic. Location of the Amarr–Minmatar frontline; the 24th Imperial Crusade and Tribal Liberation Force fight over its constellations. Recent reporting: 'The Bleak Lands back under Amarr control' marks a major Crusade win in the YC128 cycle.

nodes.name: Devoid region
nodes.brief: Faction Warfare war zone — Amarr–Minmatar frontline territory adjacent to the Bleak Lands.
nodes.master_summary: Faction Warfare war zone region — Amarr–Minmatar frontline territory adjacent to the Bleak Lands. The 24th Imperial Crusade / Tribal Liberation Force operational zone.

nodes.name: Placid region
nodes.brief: Faction Warfare war zone — Caldari–Federation frontline. Adjacent to Solitude and the YC124 Federation seizure.
nodes.master_summary: Faction Warfare war zone region — Caldari–Federation frontline. Adjacent to Solitude. The YC124 Federation seizure of Intaki + adjacent constellations occurred at the Placid–Verge Vendor border. State Protectorate / FDU operational zone.

nodes.name: Verge Vendor region
nodes.brief: Federation-controlled FW war zone. Adjacent to Placid; site of YC124 Federation expansion.
nodes.master_summary: Federation-controlled FW war zone adjacent to Placid. Site of YC124 Federation expansion that triggered the Uprising arc.

nodes.name: Metropolis region
nodes.brief: Minmatar Republic region adjacent to Heimatar. Tribal homelands and Republic Fleet operational base.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar Republic region adjacent to Heimatar. Contains tribal homelands and Republic Fleet operational base. Brutor / Sebiestor heritage worlds sit here.

nodes.name: Essence region
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation core region. Contains Caille (Federation capital) and the major Federation Senate worlds.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation core region. Contains Caille (Federation capital) and the major Federation Senate worlds. Seat of the Presidency; Aguard / Roden / Moreau succession all played out here.

nodes.name: Battle of Floseswin IV
nodes.brief: YC117 Amarr–Minmatar capital-fleet engagement above Floseswin IV. Triggered the Drifter assassination of Empress Jamyl I.
nodes.master_summary: YC117 Amarr–Minmatar capital-fleet engagement above Floseswin IV — pitched dreadnought-class warfare. Triggered the Drifter assassination of Empress Jamyl I (Drifters intervened to kill her during the battle). Floseswin marks the moment when the Drifter conflict went from background phenomenon to direct intervention in empire politics.

nodes.name: Vherokior Tribe
nodes.brief: One of the seven Minmatar tribes. Spiritual / mystic identity within the Republic's tribal pluralism.
nodes.master_summary: One of the seven Minmatar tribes restored by the Elder War cycle. Spiritual / mystic identity within the Republic's tribal pluralism — Vherokior spiritual-leader expeditions are the canonical signal of Vherokior cultural autonomy.

nodes.name: Provost-Marshal Office (Khanid Kingdom)
nodes.brief: Khanid Kingdom's military-judicial office. Allar Chakaide held the role during the YC120 Kahah pacification.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Kingdom's military-judicial office — combines military command authority with imperial-judicial powers in Khanid space. Allar Chakaide held the role during the YC120 Kahah pacification; the office is the Khanid analog of the Imperial Court chamberlainship in Domain. Aldrith Shutaq operates within the Provost-Marshal command structure.

nodes.name: Five Heirs (Amarr Royal Houses)
nodes.brief: The doctrinal Amarr-Empire structure of five Royal Houses (Sarum, Tash-Murkon, Kor-Azor, Kador, Ardishapur), each providing a Heir candidate for the throne.
nodes.master_summary: The doctrinal Amarr-Empire structure of five Royal Houses (Sarum, Tash-Murkon, Kor-Azor, Kador, Ardishapur), each providing a Heir candidate for the throne under the championship-and-succession rules. The Heideran VII → Doriam II → Karsoth-subverted-Sarum → Jamyl I → Catiz I succession ran through this structure. The Three-Layer Amarr concept (Empire / Houses / Provost-Marshal frontier) interlocks with the Five Heirs.

nodes.name: Crielere Project (collapse)
nodes.brief: YC105 Federation–State joint research project that collapsed amid Caldari sabotage allegations. Foreshadowed the modern Mountain/Ocean axis split.
nodes.master_summary: YC105 Federation–State joint research project announced as a milestone in inter-empire cooperation. Caldari police were investigated after sabotage and vandalism accusations, and the project collapsed amid intra-State factional conflict. Foreshadowed the modern Mountain/Ocean axis: pro-cooperation factions (later Ishukone-aligned) vs anti-Federation factions (later Provist/Heth-aligned).

nodes.name: Caldari State Invasion of Luminaire
nodes.brief: YC110 Caldari military strike into the Luminaire system — the Gallente capital system. Pre-Heth State military buildup; established the State's willingness to project force into Federation core space.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Caldari military strike into the Luminaire system — the Gallente capital. Pre-Heth State military buildup; established the State's willingness to project force into Federation core space. Coincided with the Tibus Heth coup at Caldari Constructions; the two events together produced the Empyrean Age State.

nodes.name: Kador Invasion of Gallente Space
nodes.brief: YC110 House Kador military operation across the Federation border. Amarr–Federation flashpoint that the Empire later disowned.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 House Kador military operation across the Federation border — an Amarr Royal House conducting unilateral military action without Imperial sanction. Empress Jamyl I subsequently reprimanded Heir Kador and confiscated their fleet. Marks the Kador house's break with Imperial central authority and the structural origin of the modern Sarum-Kador rivalry.

nodes.name: Fall of Niarja
nodes.brief: YC122 Triglavian conquest of Niarja, severing the high-sec trade corridor between Amarr and Caldari space.
nodes.master_summary: YC122 Triglavian conquest of Niarja during the Triglavian Invasion — severing the high-sec trade corridor between Amarr and Caldari space and collapsing what was historically the most heavily-trafficked sub-cluster route. The Niarja severance is the structural cause of the Amarr-Caldari trade reorientation that Catiz I's territorial reorganization later codified.

nodes.name: Publication of Pax Amarria
nodes.brief: Emperor Heideran VII's seminal philosophical book on imperial governance, religious orthodoxy, and the Reclaiming.
nodes.master_summary: Heideran VII's seminal philosophical book on imperial governance, religious orthodoxy, and the Reclaiming. Published YC105; established the modern Amarr theological consensus and was the canonical reference text Catiz I's reorganization later partially repudiated. Commonly cited in Imperial Court rhetoric and the Five Heirs succession debates.

nodes.name: Salvation Crusade
nodes.brief: YC111 Minmatar preacher Jarek's religious crusade across the Amarr–Republic border. Trans-empire religious mobilization with Heimatar firefight engagements.
nodes.master_summary: YC111 Minmatar preacher Jarek's religious crusade — a trans-empire mobilization that crossed the Amarr–Republic border. The Crusade encountered hostile ships in Heimatar with mercenary intervention; tested the Empire–Republic religious-tolerance border. Gallente medical charities came under pressure for supporting the Crusade. A Salvation-Crusade-style ideological mobilization is one structural template for the YC128 Warpath cycle.

nodes.name: Archangels Annex Skarkon (and withdraw)
nodes.brief: YC110 Angel Cartel sub-faction Archangels declared annexation of the Skarkon system; later withdrew the claim. Republic governance test case.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Angel Cartel sub-faction Archangels declared annexation of the Skarkon system, then withdrew the claim under Republic counter-pressure. The Skarkon test case became canonical reference for Republic governance failure analysis — and prefigured the later YC123-era Skarkon Angel takeover that Aurora's Skarkon node tracks.

nodes.name: General Akuras
nodes.brief: Mordu's Legion commander appointed YC120 to oversee Legion forces on Caldari Prime. Operational successor to Muryia Mordu in the Caldari Prime theater.
nodes.master_summary: Mordu's Legion commander appointed YC120 to oversee Legion forces on Caldari Prime as part of an operational reorganization. Operational successor to Muryia Mordu in the Caldari Prime theater; the appointment signaled the Legion's deeper Caldari-Prime-occupation phase before the eventual YC128-era departure.

nodes.name: Jarek (Salvation Crusader)
nodes.brief: Minmatar preacher who led the YC111 Salvation Crusade across the Amarr–Republic border.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar preacher who led the YC111 Salvation Crusade — a religious mobilization that crossed into Amarr space and sustained engagements through the Heimatar firefights. Jarek's movement is the canonical example of bottom-up trans-empire religious activism and the prototype for Ida-faith era figures (Negui Erlon, Eloa Enaro) who structurally repeat the pattern.

nodes.name: Stavros (YC105 Amarr throne pretender)
nodes.brief: Made bid for the Amarr throne during the YC105 succession trial. Notable failed challenger in the Heideran-Doriam succession cycle.
nodes.master_summary: Made bid for the Amarr throne during the YC105 succession trial. Notable failed challenger in the Heideran-Doriam succession cycle; structurally the same role as later figures who challenge the Empress's authority but lack a House backing strong enough to convert the bid.

nodes.name: Cult of Tetrimon
nodes.brief: Amarr orthodox religious sect. Investigates clone-tech for theological compliance; ran Hollow Clone Disaster investigation.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr orthodox religious sect — the doctrinal-purity wing of the Amarr theology. Investigates clone-tech for theological compliance; ran the Order of St. Tetrimon investigation into the Hollow Clone Disaster. Cult's posture is anti-capsuleer-immortality on theological grounds; Heideran VII tolerated them but Catiz I's reorganization curbed their influence.

nodes.name: Third Jovian Empire
nodes.brief: Current iteration of the Jovian polity following the Second Empire's collapse. Diminished, isolated; Construct-extracted survivors.
nodes.master_summary: Current iteration of the Jovian polity following the Second Empire's collapse. Diminished, isolated; the Construct-extracted survivors of the Second Empire form its core. Jovian Admiral Ouria's YC105 rogue defection was a Third-Empire-era crisis. Operationally inscrutable; the Drifter emergence is the cosmic-scale crisis they have not formally claimed.

nodes.name: Mountain / Ocean / Forest factional split (Caldari)
nodes.brief: The three Caldari political factions: Mountain (state-loyalist militants), Ocean (corporate cooperationists), Forest (industrial-traditionalist neutrals).
nodes.master_summary: The three Caldari political factions inside the State: Mountain (state-loyalist militants — Heth/Provists/Saraki), Ocean (corporate cooperationists — Otro/Mens/Ishukone), Forest (industrial-traditionalist neutrals — Hyasyoda/Lai Dai). The Crielere Project YC105 was the Ocean-faction signature initiative; its collapse marked Mountain ascendancy. The current YC128 Saraki consolidation is structurally a Mountain reassertion against Ocean tendencies.

nodes.name: Tovil-Toba
nodes.brief: Caldari capsuleer who crashed her Raven into Caldari Prime — the legendary act that ended the original Caldari Rebellion war. Liberation Day commemorates her sacrifice.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari capsuleer who crashed her Raven into Caldari Prime — the legendary act that ended the original Caldari Rebellion war and gave the Caldari people their homeworld. The Hueromont casualties from that strike are commemorated annually; modern Caldari Liberation Day is the political descendant of her sacrifice. The structural template for capsuleer-as-political-actor that the YC128 cycle inherits.

nodes.name: Assassination of Emperor Doriam II
nodes.brief: YC106 destruction of an Imperial transport carrying Doriam II's retainers. Triggered the succession crisis that produced Karsoth's regency.
nodes.master_summary: YC106 destruction of an Imperial transport carrying Doriam II's retainers — Doriam II killed in the strike. Triggered the succession crisis that produced Court Chamberlain Dochuta Karsoth's regency. The succession theft by Karsoth is the structural origin of the Sarum-claim subversion arc that continued into Jamyl I's reign.

nodes.name: Aidonis Award to Heideran VII
nodes.brief: Heideran VII received the Aidonis (peace-prize) award. Subsequent peace-prize-related murder and mayhem cycle exposed Empire–Federation tensions.
nodes.master_summary: YC104 Aidonis (peace-prize) award to Heideran VII — controversial Federation honor for an Amarr Emperor whose Empire still practiced slavery. The award sparked a cycle of murder and mayhem that exposed Empire–Federation civilizational tensions. Heideran's tolerance of the Pax Amarria reformist position grew from this period.

nodes.name: Dr. Tukoss
nodes.brief: Drifter-research scientist whose death was confirmed by DED YC117. Central figure in the Drifter-Sleeper-Construct research thread.
nodes.master_summary: Drifter-research scientist whose death was confirmed by DED YC117. Central figure in the Drifter-Sleeper-Construct research thread; his work bridged AIR Labs and Sisters of EVE Anoikis programs. Loss of his expertise pushed AIR Labs into the post-YC117 reorganization that culminated in the Exordium charter.

nodes.name: Battle of Mekhios
nodes.brief: YC110 Amarr–Minmatar engagement at Mekhios; subsequent disaster compounded the casualty cycle.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Amarr–Minmatar capital-fleet engagement at Mekhios. In the wake of the battle, a disaster struck the system — compounding the casualty cycle. Mekhios joins Floseswin and Sarum Prime in the Amarr-Republic conflict's signature battles list; together they define the Imperian Age military theater.

nodes.name: Jovian Admiral Ouria Defection
nodes.brief: YC105 Jovian Admiral Ouria went rogue — a public Jove military defection. Defeated and pursued; canonical signal of Third Empire-era internal instability.
nodes.master_summary: YC105 Jovian Admiral Ouria went rogue — a public Jove military defection rare enough to make inter-empire news. Defeated and pursued by Jove forces; canonical signal of Third Empire-era internal instability that the Drifter emergence later magnified.

nodes.name: Caroline Grace
nodes.brief: Capsuleer who detected the YC116 stellar anomaly that became known as Caroline's Star.
nodes.master_summary: Capsuleer who detected the YC116 stellar anomaly that became known as Caroline's Star — the visible signature of the Drifter network reaching New Eden's normal space. Her detection is the moment the Drifter conflict moved from Anoikis backstory to public phenomenon.

nodes.name: Ushra'Khan
nodes.brief: Minmatar capsuleer alliance. Anti-slavery, pro-Republic. Captured the Imperial Armageddon YC107 — major capsuleer-faction propaganda victory.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar capsuleer alliance. Anti-slavery, pro-Republic — the structural Minmatar-side capsuleer faction that mirrors the Empire's pro-slavery capsuleer alliances. Captured the Imperial Armageddon YC107, a major capsuleer-faction propaganda victory. Endorsed Maleatu Shakor's elevation to Sanmatar; protested the Insorum controversy.

nodes.name: Roden Shipyards
nodes.brief: Federation shipbuilder. Granted extensive Federation construction contracts YC105; built the first privately owned Gallente vessel YC107.
nodes.master_summary: Federation shipbuilder. Granted extensive Federation construction contracts YC105 (with competitors crying foul); built the first privately owned Gallente Navy-spec vessel YC107. The Roden family's commercial success underwrote Soraya Roden's later YC128 presidential candidacy — making this corp the structural backstory for the YC128 capsuleer-influence-election cycle.

nodes.name: Caldari Constructions Workers Revolt
nodes.brief: Tibus Heth's labor-organizing coup at Caldari Constructions — the workers' revolt that put him on the path to Provost.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 labor-organizing coup at Caldari Constructions led by Tibus Heth, a foreman-turned-revolutionary. The Mercantile Club hosted discussion on the takeover; the Federation cast wary eye on 'the Heth situation.' This is the structural origin of Heth's rise — pre-Malkalen, pre-Empyrean Age. Without Caldari Constructions, no Provist regime.

nodes.name: Outer Ring Excavations (ORE)
nodes.brief: Major Caldari mining megacorp. Founded YC91 by Yani Sar Arteu. Upwell Consortium founding member.
nodes.master_summary: Major Caldari mining megacorp founded YC91 by Yani Sar Arteu. Upwell Consortium founding member; controls the largest mining vessel construction and operates Outer Ring station infrastructure. ORE membership of Upwell was canonically confirmed by Yani Sar Arteu in the aftermath of the Citadel-era station seizures (YC117). Operationally neutral within the State, but Forest-faction-aligned.

nodes.name: Yani Sar Arteu
nodes.brief: Founder of ORE. Confirmed ORE membership in Upwell Consortium during the YC117 Citadel-era station seizures.
nodes.master_summary: Founder of Outer Ring Excavations (YC91). Confirmed ORE membership in Upwell Consortium during the YC117 Citadel-era station seizures — a canonical statement that locked the Upwell-megacorp alignment graph. Reclusive industrial leader; minimal political exposure outside ORE business.

nodes.name: Carthum Conglomerate
nodes.brief: Amarr arms manufacturer. Upwell Consortium founding member. Major Amarr industrial player.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr arms manufacturer; Upwell Consortium founding member. Major industrial player on the Amarr side of the four-empire industrial split. Occasional earnings drops drive Amarr stock-market reporting (YC110 below-average-earnings cycle). The Amarr counterpart in Upwell to Ishukone/Lai Dai/Kaalakiota on the Caldari side.

nodes.name: CreoDron
nodes.brief: Federation drone manufacturer. Upwell Consortium founding member. Major Federation industrial player.
nodes.master_summary: Federation drone manufacturer; Upwell Consortium founding member. Major Federation industrial player on the Gallente side of the four-empire industrial split. YC109 investment slump signaled the broader Federation pre-Heth slowdown.

nodes.name: Egonics Inc.
nodes.brief: Caldari electronics company. Founded YC54. Upwell Consortium founding member.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari electronics company founded YC54. Upwell Consortium founding member. The smallest of the Upwell founding signatories; provides electronic systems and subcomponents.

nodes.name: Society of Conscious Thought (SoCT)
nodes.brief: Inter-empire research society. Capsuleer briefings on Drifter / Triglavian / Construct findings; bridges empires on cosmic-scale phenomena.
nodes.master_summary: Inter-empire research society. Convenes capsuleer SoCT briefings on Drifter / Triglavian / Construct findings; functions as the structural bridge between the four empires on cosmic-scale research that no single empire wants to claim. Its briefings at Yulai are canonical reference points for the Drifter Crisis aftermath. Adjacent to AIR Labs and the Sisters of EVE Anoikis programs.

nodes.name: Zorya Triglav
nodes.brief: Senior Triglavian voice. Took over capsuleer billboards YC121 — first widespread public Triglavian communication.
nodes.master_summary: Senior Triglavian voice. Took over capsuleer billboards YC121 in a coordinated communication event — first widespread public Triglavian appearance in inter-empire media. Continues to surface as Triglavian-side speaker through the Pochven and Drifter-conflict cycles.

nodes.name: Triglavian Collective Revealed
nodes.brief: YC120 first major public revelation of the Triglavian Collective as a third-pole civilization. Two years before the Invasion.
nodes.master_summary: YC120 first major public revelation of the Triglavian Collective as a coherent civilization rather than scattered abyssal anomalies. The Scope reported the canonical introduction. Two-year warning before the YC122 Triglavian Invasion that conquered Niarja and the 27 systems forming Pochven.

nodes.name: Minmatar Elders Invasion of Amarr Empire
nodes.brief: YC110 Minmatar Elder-led invasion deep into Amarr space. Companion event to the Caldari Luminaire invasion same year.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Minmatar Elder-led invasion deep into Amarr space, including the Sarum Prime strike and the Mekhios theater. Companion event to the Caldari Luminaire invasion the same year — together they define the 'YC110 wars' decade and triggered the Empyrean Age. The Elder Fleet's eventual retreat in Kor-Azor region marked the end of the Republic's deep-strike capability and led to Jamyl's superweapon deployment that cemented her reign.

nodes.name: Hilen Tukoss Defection
nodes.brief: YC113 defection of Caldari scientist Hilen Tukoss. Pivotal moment in the Drifter / Sleeper / Construct research arc; predates his YC117 confirmed death.
nodes.master_summary: YC113 defection of Caldari scientist Hilen Tukoss. Pivotal moment in the Drifter / Sleeper / Construct research arc — Tukoss carried Anoikis-research expertise out of Caldari corporate confinement. His defection prefigured the SoE / AIR Labs research consolidation that the Exordium charter eventually formalized. He was confirmed dead YC117.

nodes.name: Capsuleer Alliances
nodes.brief: YC106 formal recognition of capsuleer-organized alliances as sovereign-equivalent political bodies. Origin of Goonswarm, Test, and the modern null-sec power blocs.
nodes.master_summary: YC106 formal recognition of capsuleer-organized alliances as sovereign-equivalent political bodies — the legal basis for capsuleer-led sov-null and the structural complement to the empires' Faction Warfare militias. Goonswarm, Test, and the modern null-sec power blocs all date their existence from this recognition cycle. The recognition is upstream of every YC110+ capsuleer-faction story arc including Ushra'Khan and the Imperial Armageddon capture.

nodes.name: Jacus Roden
nodes.brief: Federation President from YC111 cycle. Roden Shipyards family — capsuleer-friendly Federation candidate.
nodes.master_summary: Federation President from the YC111 election cycle. Roden Shipyards family — capsuleer-friendly Federation candidate; structural ancestor of the Soraya Roden YC128 candidacy. The Roden political dynasty connects industrial capital to the Federation presidency.

nodes.name: Souro Foiritan
nodes.brief: Federation President during Heideran VII / Crielere era. Concerned about Heideran's health post-Aidonis cycle.
nodes.master_summary: Federation President during the Heideran VII / Crielere era. Concerned about Heideran's health post-Aidonis cycle. Pre-Heth-era diplomat who navigated the Federation-Empire-State triangle before the YC110 wars decade upended it.

nodes.name: Jin-Mei
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation bloodline. Agriculturally-focused; intersected the YC110 FedMart agricultural intervention cycle.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation bloodline; one of the four Gallente sub-bloodlines (Gallente / Intaki / Mannar / Jin-Mei). Agriculturally-focused; intersected the YC110 FedMart agricultural-intervention cycle. Aguard's pre-presidency campaigning rallies targeted the Jin-Mei vote.

nodes.name: Nugoeihuvi Corporation (NOH)
nodes.brief: Caldari entertainment / media megacorp. Forest-faction-aligned. Mind Clash league sponsor.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari entertainment / media megacorp. Forest-faction-aligned within the State political system. Mind Clash league sponsor; Kaalakiota–NOH qualifier matches are the highest-rated cultural events in the State. Operates the Ev0ke trademark dispute cycle. The NOH-Kaalakiota-Wiyrkomi triangle is the structural cultural axis underneath the Mountain/Ocean/Forest political split.

nodes.name: Imperial Armageddon (capsuleer trophy)
nodes.brief: Amarr battleship captured by Ushra'Khan capsuleer alliance YC107. Major capsuleer-faction propaganda victory.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr battleship captured by the Ushra'Khan capsuleer alliance in YC107. The capture is the canonical capsuleer-faction propaganda victory of the era; established the precedent that capsuleer alliances can humiliate empire militaries through coordinated action. Modern null-sec pop culture continues to reference the Armageddon capture.

nodes.name: Sansha Incursions (YC112)
nodes.brief: YC112 Sansha invasion underway across high-sec. Markets affected by Nation raids; first major sustained Sansha cycle of the modern era.
nodes.master_summary: YC112 sustained Sansha incursion cycle — Sansha's Nation strikes at the empires across high-sec, market disruption in Luminaire and Empire core systems. Show of force brought over 1,000 capsuleer pilots to Nation space in response. First major sustained Sansha cycle of the modern era; structural prototype for the Triglavian Invasion that followed YC122.

nodes.name: Dread Guristas
nodes.brief: Elite Guristas sub-faction. Conducted YC117 daring raid that stole a supercarrier — major capsuleer-era pirate operation.
nodes.master_summary: Elite Guristas sub-faction. Conducted YC117 daring raid that stole a supercarrier — the highest-tier piracy of the capsuleer era. Operationally distinct from rank-and-file Guristas; the Dread sub-faction handles capital-class operations and high-value strategic raids.

nodes.name: World Ark (Triglavian flagship)
nodes.brief: Triglavian flagship-class vessel. Deployed in pre-Invasion warning phase YC121. Carries the Triglavian conquest doctrine to target systems.
nodes.master_summary: Triglavian flagship-class vessel. Deployed in pre-Invasion warning phase YC121 (impending-invasion announcement). Carries the Triglavian conquest doctrine to target systems; canonical example of cross-civilizational hardware exposure during the Invasion cycle. Each World Ark deployment was a major news event.

nodes.name: Aidonis Award to Alexander Noir
nodes.brief: Alexander Noir received the Aidonis (peace-prize) award in YC110 — the same year his Nyx struck Ishukone HQ at Malkalen.
nodes.master_summary: Alexander Noir received the Aidonis (peace-prize) award in YC110 — the same year his Nyx mothership struck Ishukone HQ at Malkalen and killed Otro Gariushi. The structural ambiguity: Noir was being publicly honored as the Federation's peace champion at the moment the broker plot used him for the Empyrean Age's signature assassination. Whether Noir was set up or willingly participated is canonically unresolved.

nodes.name: Intaki system
nodes.brief: Intaki bloodline homeworld. Federation system seized YC124. Site of Lost Adama religious arc and Ishukone development rights.
nodes.master_summary: Intaki bloodline homeworld system. Federation member system seized YC124 in the Federation Navy invasion that triggered the Uprising arc. Site of the YC120 Lost Adama religious arc and the Ishukone development rights deal that gave Mordu's Legion full military access. The Intaki Syndicate's name origin — exiles from this system founded the dissident-exile federation.

nodes.name: Inner Zone Shipping
nodes.brief: Federation logistics megacorp. Major shipping company — backbone of Federation trade and a structural pillar of the Federation industrial economy.
nodes.master_summary: Federation logistics megacorp. Major shipping company — backbone of Federation trade and a structural pillar of the Federation industrial economy. The Federation analog of Ishukone Watch in the State or Carthum Conglomerate in the Empire — a logistics company that doubles as a polity-of-influence within the Federation Senate.

nodes.name: Caldari (bloodline)
nodes.brief: Caldari humans — the Civire and Deteis sub-bloodlines under Achura ancillary integration. Cultural foundation of the Caldari State.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari humans — the Civire (warrior-pragmatic) and Deteis (mercantile-philosophical) sub-bloodlines under Achura (spiritual-monastic) ancillary integration. Cultural foundation of the Caldari State; descendant of the original colonists who became culturally distinct from Gallente during the pre-Rebellion era. Tovil-Toba's sacrifice made her a structural ancestor of the modern bloodline-State alignment.

nodes.name: Amarr (bloodline)
nodes.brief: Amarr humans — Holder caste under Imperial doctrine. Theological-imperial; the Reclaiming-bound bloodline.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr humans — Holder caste under Imperial doctrine. Theological-imperial cultural foundation; the Reclaiming-bound bloodline whose philosophical apparatus produced Pax Amarria. Stratified into Royal Houses (Sarum/Tash-Murkon/Kor-Azor/Kador/Ardishapur) and the Khanid sub-bloodline. The Empire's identity derives from this bloodline's mission.

nodes.name: Minmatar (bloodline)
nodes.brief: Minmatar humans — seven-tribes structure (Brutor / Sebiestor / Krusual / Vherokior / Nefantar / Starkmanir / Thukker). Republic cultural foundation.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar humans — seven-tribes cultural structure (Brutor / Sebiestor / Krusual / Vherokior / Nefantar / Starkmanir / Thukker). Republic cultural foundation; the people that the Amarr Reclaiming subjugated and the Minmatar Rebellion freed. Tribal Council is the modern political surface of the bloodline's pre-Empire structure.

nodes.name: Gallente (bloodline)
nodes.brief: Gallente humans — the Federation's founding bloodline. Liberal-democratic cultural foundation.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente humans — the Federation's founding bloodline. Liberal-democratic cultural foundation; integrated with Intaki, Mannar, and Jin-Mei sub-bloodlines into the Federation. The bloodline's cultural-Gallente integration policy was the trigger for the Caldari Rebellion. Federal Defense Union and Federation Navy operate under Gallente cultural assumptions.

nodes.name: Civire (Caldari sub-bloodline)
nodes.brief: Caldari sub-bloodline. Warrior-pragmatic; the State's military caste origin.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari sub-bloodline. Warrior-pragmatic culture; the State's military caste origin. Most Caldari Navy personnel and State Protectorate FW militia members are Civire. Tibus Heth's labor-militancy template tracks Civire cultural assumptions.

nodes.name: Deteis (Caldari sub-bloodline)
nodes.brief: Caldari sub-bloodline. Mercantile-philosophical; the State's executive and corporate caste origin.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari sub-bloodline. Mercantile-philosophical culture; the State's executive and corporate caste origin. Most CEP members and megacorp executives (Otro / Mens / Tibus Heth's eventual rivals) are Deteis. The Lai-Dai-vs-Ishukone axis is structurally a Deteis-internal philosophical split.

nodes.name: Achura (Caldari sub-bloodline)
nodes.brief: Caldari sub-bloodline. Spiritual-monastic; integrated into the State as ancillary cultural reservoir.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari sub-bloodline. Spiritual-monastic culture; integrated into the State as ancillary cultural reservoir. Sani Sabik researchers and Construct-adjacent thinkers disproportionately come from Achura backgrounds.

nodes.name: Mannar (Gallente sub-bloodline)
nodes.brief: Gallente sub-bloodline. Athletic-traditionalist; integrated into Federation cultural pluralism.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente sub-bloodline. Athletic-traditionalist culture; one of the four sub-bloodlines integrated into Federation cultural pluralism (Gallente / Intaki / Mannar / Jin-Mei).

nodes.name: Khanid (Amarr sub-bloodline)
nodes.brief: Amarr sub-bloodline. Aristocratic-militaristic. Founded the Khanid Kingdom secession from the Empire.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr sub-bloodline. Aristocratic-militaristic culture. Founded the Khanid Kingdom secession from the Empire (Khanid II). Operationally autonomous within the Amarr civilizational sphere; runs the Royal Khanid Navy and Provost-Marshal Office.

nodes.name: Avatar-class Titan (Amarr)
