nodes.brief: Amarr Empire's Titan-class capital ship. Honor-guard role at imperial successions and major ceremonial events.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire's Titan-class capital ship. Honor-guard role at imperial successions (Avatars escorted Succession Trials champions YC118) and major ceremonial events. Class deployed in Reclaiming-era operations; canonical Amarr capital-tier asset.

nodes.name: Erebus-class Titan (Gallente)
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation Titan-class capital ship. Class destroyed in the YC110 Goonswarm Etherium Reach incident.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation Titan-class capital ship. Class destroyed in the YC110 Goonswarm Titan-destruction incident in Etherium Reach — an early demonstration of capsuleer-alliance capital-tier capability surpassing empire militaries.

nodes.name: Ragnarok-class Titan (Minmatar)
nodes.brief: Minmatar Republic Titan-class capital ship. Mercenary Coalition Titan destroyed YC109 — capsuleer-era Titan combat precedent.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar Republic Titan-class capital ship. Mercenary Coalition Titan destroyed YC109 — capsuleer-era Titan-combat precedent. The Elder Fleet's deepest strikes during the Minmatar Elders Invasion deployed Ragnarok-class assets.

nodes.name: Nyx-class Mothership (Gallente)
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation Mothership-class capital ship. The Malkalen-strike vessel piloted by Alexander Noir; Federation Mothership stolen YC108.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation Mothership-class capital ship. The Malkalen-strike vessel piloted by Alexander Noir in the YC110 disaster that killed Otro Gariushi. Federation Mothership stolen YC108 in a CONCORD-shipping incident — the prefigure for the broker plot's Noir vessel acquisition.

nodes.name: Capsuleer Day (annual)
nodes.brief: Annual capsuleer-recognition holiday. Established YC117 by Upwell Consortium; the recognition cycle for capsuleer political importance.
nodes.master_summary: Annual capsuleer-recognition holiday established YC117 by Upwell Consortium. Capsuleer Day XXIII (YC128) coincides with the Warpath Protocol failure cycle — the first Capsuleer Day during a CONCORD-system breach since the YC110 Yulai gate event. The recognition cycle for capsuleer political importance; structural ancestor of the YC128 Warpath capsuleer mobilization.

nodes.name: Federation Day (annual)
nodes.brief: Annual Federation founding holiday. Cultural anchor of Federation identity.
nodes.master_summary: Annual Gallente Federation founding holiday. Cultural anchor of Federation identity; presidential addresses traditionally fall during the Federation Day cycle. Structural complement to Caldari Liberation Day and Republic Liberation Day.

nodes.name: Republic Liberation Day (annual)
nodes.brief: Annual Minmatar Republic holiday commemorating the original Rebellion. Republic stands still for the day.
nodes.master_summary: Annual Minmatar Republic holiday commemorating the original Minmatar Rebellion. The Republic stands still for the day — schools, government, and tribal-council operations all pause. Cultural anchor parallel to Federation Day and Caldari Liberation Day; Tribal Council uses the day for major proclamations.

nodes.name: Caldari Liberation Day (annual)
nodes.brief: Annual Caldari State holiday commemorating Tovil-Toba's sacrifice — the liberation of Caldari Prime.
nodes.master_summary: Annual Caldari State holiday commemorating Tovil-Toba's sacrifice — the capsuleer who crashed her Raven into Caldari Prime to liberate the homeworld. Cultural anchor of the State; CEP and Caldari Navy use the day for major proclamations. The Hueromont casualties from her strike are commemorated alongside the liberation.

nodes.name: The Empyrean Age (era)
nodes.brief: YC105+ era of capsuleer political dominance. Begins with Pax Amarria publication and Crielere Project; defines the four-empire system through YC128.
nodes.master_summary: The YC105+ era of capsuleer political dominance — opens with Pax Amarria publication, Crielere Project announcement, and the rise of Tibus Heth at Caldari Constructions; runs through the Malkalen Disaster, YC112 Caldari Prime invasion, the Drifter Crisis, the Triglavian Invasion, and the YC128 Warpath cycle. The era defines the four-empire system as it currently exists. Tony Gonzales's Templar One book is set in the early Empyrean Age.

nodes.name: Pax Amarria Doctrine
nodes.brief: Heideran VII's theological-political consensus from his YC105 book. Tolerant Reclaiming; reformist Empire.
nodes.master_summary: Heideran VII's theological-political consensus from his YC105 book Pax Amarria — a tolerant Reclaiming and reformist Empire posture. Established the Heideran-era Amarr Empire's relationship with the Federation and the Republic. Catiz I's territorial reorganization partially repudiated the doctrine; the Cult of Tetrimon opposed it on orthodox-purity grounds. Modern Amarr political debates reference the Pax Amarria-vs-orthodoxy axis.

nodes.name: Lord Sansha Kuvakei II
nodes.brief: The resurrected Sansha Kuvakei. Unknown whether truly the original or a constructed simulacrum. Coordinates Sansha incursions from Stain.
nodes.master_summary: The resurrected Sansha Kuvakei. Unknown whether truly the original (technologically resurrected via True-Slave-Master tech) or a constructed simulacrum operating in his name. Coordinates Sansha incursions from Stain region. The structural prototype for the Deathless framing — both are 'resurrected immortal antagonist' figures running centralized merger networks. The YC112 resurrection-truth-or-trickery cycle established the canonical ambiguity.

nodes.name: The Servitor (Jamyl I superweapon)
nodes.brief: Amarr superweapon used by Empress Jamyl I to destroy the Elder Fleet at Sarum Prime. Jovian-derived; dramatic restoration of Imperial power.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr superweapon used by Empress Jamyl I to destroy the Elder Fleet during the YC110 Sarum Prime strike. Believed to be Jovian-derived (imperial-jovian cooperation pre-dates the Third Empire's isolation). The superweapon's use ended the Minmatar Elder Fleet threat and cemented Jamyl's reign — but its origin and consumption are canonically opaque, fueling later Imperial-Court speculation about Jove-Empire arrangements.

nodes.name: Three Quintons (Triglavian Collective sub-factions)
nodes.brief: The three Triglavian sub-factional clades: Bujan, Liminal Tide, Veles. Each runs sub-portions of the Pochven conquest doctrine.
nodes.master_summary: The three Triglavian sub-factional clades: Bujan, Liminal Tide, Veles. Each runs sub-portions of the Pochven conquest doctrine and the wider abyssal-front operations. Triglavian internal politics surface through the Three Quintons split; capsuleer briefings on Triglavian intent must triangulate which Quinton speaks. Zorya Triglav speaks across all three.

nodes.name: Marcus Jror
nodes.brief: Templar protagonist of Tony Gonzales's Empyrean Age / Templar One novels. Caldari capsuleer-clone-soldier hybrid.
nodes.master_summary: Templar protagonist of Tony Gonzales's Empyrean Age / Templar One novels. Caldari capsuleer-clone-soldier hybrid; the clone-soldier program's first widely documented operative. Operates within the Mens Reppola / Muryia Mordu / Insorum nexus — protects Insorum production at Mens's mandate. The canonical instance of clone-soldier tech in narrative form.

nodes.name: Vince Barthelo
nodes.brief: Templar One character — Caldari clone-soldier who recovered Insorum during the broker plot. Templar program operative.
nodes.master_summary: Templar One character — Caldari clone-soldier who recovered Insorum during the broker plot, the operative whose 'recovery' became the narrative engine of the post-Otro Insorum continuation. Templar program operative directly tied to Mens Reppola's mandate and Mordu's Legion oversight.

nodes.name: Kresh (Brutor Tribal Chief)
nodes.brief: Brutor Tribal Chief on the Tribal Council. Maleatu Shakor's tribal counterpart on the Council.
nodes.master_summary: Brutor Tribal Chief on the Tribal Council. Maleatu Shakor's tribal counterpart — Shakor is Brutor by tribe but Sanmatar by office, so Kresh runs Brutor tribal affairs day-to-day. Voice of Brutor warrior-pragmatism on the Council.

nodes.name: Drifter battleship
nodes.brief: Drifter capital-class warship. Heavy hull design with gravity-warhead weaponry; protagonist of Drifter-faction combat.
nodes.master_summary: Drifter capital-class warship. Heavy hull design with gravity-warhead weaponry derived from drifter hyperspace tech. Protagonist of Drifter-faction combat across the Drifter Crisis and the Triglavian-Drifter conflict. Killed Empress Jamyl I at Floseswin IV.

nodes.name: Sleeper Drone
nodes.brief: Autonomous drone-class entities operating in Anoikis wormhole space. Originally Sleeper-civilization assets; awakened during Apocrypha.
nodes.master_summary: Autonomous drone-class entities operating in Anoikis wormhole space. Originally Sleeper-civilization defensive assets; awakened during the YC111 Apocrypha-era wormhole opening. Capsuleers harvest Sleeper components for advanced ship modules — the SCC commodity market for Sleeper components is a structural pillar of capsuleer economy.

nodes.name: Mass Driver Strike on Starkmanir Prime
nodes.brief: The orbital bombardment that annihilated Starkmanir Prime. Idonis Ardishapur ordered; effective Starkmanir genocide.
nodes.master_summary: The orbital bombardment via mass driver that annihilated Starkmanir Prime — the Ardishapur-ordered effective Starkmanir genocide. The structural origin of the lost-tribe narrative the YC110 discovery later reversed. Reference event for the Imperian Age starkmanir-reveal cycle.

nodes.name: Arrach Sarum
nodes.brief: Heir of House Sarum. Surfaces in YC121 Triglavian-billboard cycle as senior Imperial figure.
nodes.master_summary: Heir of House Sarum. Surfaces in the YC121 Triglavian-billboard cycle as a senior Imperial figure responding to the Triglavian threat. The Sarum-claim through-line that connects Doriam succession era (Karsoth subverted) to Jamyl I's Sarum-prime-superweapon era to the YC121 Triglavian response cycle.

nodes.name: Sani Sabik (heretical sect)
nodes.brief: Blood-cult heretical Amarr sect. Theological foundation of the Blood Raider Covenant. Believes in blood as sacrament.
nodes.master_summary: Blood-cult heretical Amarr sect — theological foundation of the Blood Raider Covenant. Believes in blood as sacrament; the doctrinal complement of orthodox Amarr Reclaiming. Cardinal Omir Sarikusa's YC106 blood-quest declaration was a Sani Sabik mobilization. Achura sub-bloodline thinkers disproportionately convert to Sani Sabik.

nodes.name: Theology Council (Amarr)
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire's religious-doctrinal authority. Coordinates with Imperial Court on succession and religious-civic matters.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire's religious-doctrinal authority. Coordinates with the Imperial Court on succession and religious-civic matters; declared the official day of mourning for Malkalen victims YC110. Operates parallel to the Cult of Tetrimon — Tetrimon is the orthodox-purity wing, Theology Council is the institutional consensus.

nodes.name: Federation Senate
nodes.brief: Federation governance body. Hawkish vs Autonomist factional split surfaces in Moreau era.
nodes.master_summary: Federation governance body. Hawkish vs Autonomist factional split surfaces in the Moreau era — hawks worry the Autonomist president is unable to direct the Federation Navy firmly. Senate operates from Caille; presidential candidate rallies (Aguard, Roden, Moreau) are oriented to Senate-faction politics.

nodes.name: GenoTech Systems
nodes.brief: Caldari biotech subsidiary. YC110 drug trial horror admission.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari biotech subsidiary. YC110 drug trial horror admission — the State pharma scandal that demonstrated the unaccountability of corporate research within the Heth-era Provist regime. Wiyrkomi-aligned per the YC110 industry coverage.

nodes.name: Zainou Biotech
nodes.brief: Caldari biotech corp. YC107 security breach; YC121 Semiki biohazard containment failure.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari biotech corp. YC107 security breach; YC121 Semiki biohazard containment failure — Lai Dai under suspicion in the contamination cycle. Operates the Caldari capsuleer-implant supply chain. Zainou implants are mainstays of capsuleer fitting markets.

nodes.name: Innovaatio Varustukset (defunct)
nodes.brief: Caldari military development corp. YC109 scandal ended with sale and ruin.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari military development corp. YC109 scandal ended with sale and ruin during a multi-corp bidding war for its assets. The episode prefigured the Heth-era consolidation pattern — Caldari corporate failures get absorbed by state-favored megacorps (Kaalakiota in this case) under Provist authority.

nodes.name: Eifyr & Co
nodes.brief: Inter-empire biotech. Tukoss-research partnership; Chemal Tech collaboration.
nodes.master_summary: Inter-empire biotech. Tukoss-research partnership — issued the YC116 messaging response to Hilen Tukoss communications. Chemal Tech collaboration raised suspicions YC109 (cross-corp tech transfer). Operates between the four-empire industrial sphere — analog of Inner Zone Shipping for biotech.

nodes.name: Outer Ring region
nodes.brief: Sov-null region. ORE home territory; rare ore reserves.
nodes.master_summary: Sov-null region. ORE (Outer Ring Excavations) home territory; rare ore reserves and capsuleer-alliance industrial extraction zones. Adjacent to Cloud Ring and Curse.

nodes.name: Cloud Ring region
nodes.brief: Sov-null region. Capsuleer-alliance contested territory; major null-sec power-bloc battleground.
nodes.master_summary: Sov-null region. Capsuleer-alliance contested territory; major null-sec power-bloc battleground. Sits between Outer Ring and Pure Blind. Mordu's Legion's Pureblind position-strengthening YC107 was Cloud-Ring-adjacent.

nodes.name: Insmother region
nodes.brief: Sov-null Drone-region. Capsuleer-alliance contested territory east of Geminate.
nodes.master_summary: Sov-null Drone-region east of Geminate. Capsuleer-alliance contested territory; the Drone-region bestiary makes it unique among null-sec regions. Adjacent to the Goonswarm-Etherium Reach territory where the YC110 Erebus-titan destruction occurred.

nodes.name: Kor-Azor Cathedral Incident
nodes.brief: YC106 incident at the cathedral during the Tash-Murkon renovation debate. Aritcio Kor-Azor implicated.
nodes.master_summary: YC106 incident at the Tash-Murkon-renovation cathedral with Aritcio Kor-Azor implicated. Followed the Catiz Tash-Murkon controversy over renovation plans and the Kador escalation. Marker event in the post-Doriam succession friction between the Five Heir houses.

nodes.name: Innovaatio Varustukset Scandal
nodes.brief: YC109 Caldari military-dev corp scandal ending with sale and ruin. Multi-corp bidding war that exposed Provist consolidation pattern.
nodes.master_summary: YC109 Caldari military-development corp scandal ending with sale and ruin. Multi-corp bidding war (Kaalakiota / Wiyrkomi / Sukuuvestaa) that exposed the Heth-era Provist consolidation pattern of state-favored megacorp absorption.

nodes.name: Zainou Biotech Security Breach
nodes.brief: YC107 security breach at Zainou Biotech. Pre-Empyrean-Age corporate-security incident in the Caldari biotech sector.
nodes.master_summary: YC107 security breach at Zainou Biotech. Pre-Empyrean-Age corporate-security incident in the Caldari biotech sector; structurally part of the prefiguring pattern (Crielere collapse YC105 + Innovaatio scandal YC109 + Zainou breach YC107) that shaped the Provist consolidation justification.

nodes.name: Semiki Biohazard Containment Failure
nodes.brief: YC121 biohazard containment failure at Semiki — Lai Dai under suspicion. Late Empyrean-Age State pharma scandal.
nodes.master_summary: YC121 biohazard containment failure at Semiki — Lai Dai under suspicion in the contamination cycle. Late Empyrean-Age State pharma scandal that paralleled the YC120 Kahah weaponized-Death-Glow incident structurally — both are State-side pharma containment failures the Federation and Republic flagged as inter-empire safety crises.

nodes.name: Amarr Prime
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire capital system (Amarr system, Domain region). Imperial throneworld.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire capital system in Domain region. The Imperial throneworld; site of all Amarr coronations and major court events. Avatar-class titans honor-guard at Amarr Prime during Succession Trials. Theology Council and Imperial Court are seated here.

nodes.name: Gallente Prime
nodes.brief: Federation capital system (Luminaire). Founding world of the Federation.
nodes.master_summary: Federation capital system in the Luminaire system, Essence region. Founding world of the Federation; seat of the Senate at Caille. Site of the YC110 Caldari Luminaire invasion strike — the State military move into Federation core space that prefigured the Empyrean Age.

nodes.name: Pure Blind region
nodes.brief: Sov-null region north of Caldari space. Mordu's Legion stronghold; capsuleer-alliance battleground.
nodes.master_summary: Sov-null region north of Caldari space. Mordu's Legion's stated 'Pureblind position' (YC107 strengthening); capsuleer-alliance battleground adjacent to Cloud Ring and the Caldari Black Rise FW war zone. Operationally a major null-sec entry point.

nodes.name: Aridia region
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire frontier region. Lowsec-adjacent; site of pirate-faction operations and post-Doriam friction.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire frontier region. Lowsec-adjacent; site of pirate-faction operations and structural friction between Amarr core and the Khanid sphere. Aridia is one of the regions where the Cult of Tetrimon's orthodoxy patrols clashed with capsuleer logistics during the YC110-115 era.

nodes.name: CBD Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari megacorp (CBD Corporation). Considered foreign investment expansion YC110.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari megacorp. Considered further foreign investment YC110 alongside Lai Dai. Operates in the Caldari logistics and capital-goods sector; one of the eight Caldari megacorps. Forest-faction-aligned per the Heth-era political mapping.

nodes.name: Crimson Harvest (annual)
nodes.brief: Annual Blood Raider mobilization event. Death Glow Hunters appear; canonical pirate-faction recurring event.
nodes.master_summary: Annual Blood Raider mobilization event. Death Glow Hunters appear; canonical pirate-faction recurring event running every year. Structural complement to the Sansha Incursions and Triglavian skirmishes — the recurring pirate-faction calendar that capsuleer alliances structure their year around.

nodes.name: Northern Coalition (capsuleer alliance)
nodes.brief: Major capsuleer-alliance bloc. Northern null-sec power, organized post-YC106 capsuleer-alliance recognition.
nodes.master_summary: Major capsuleer-alliance bloc. Northern null-sec power, organized after the YC106 capsuleer-alliance recognition. Operates across the northern null-sec regions (Pure Blind, Cloud Ring, Tribute, Vale of the Silent). One of the structural counterweights to Goonswarm in the modern null-sec balance.

nodes.name: Mercenary Coalition (capsuleer alliance)
nodes.brief: Major capsuleer mercenary alliance. Lost Ragnarok-class Titan YC109 — capsuleer-Titan combat precedent.
nodes.master_summary: Major capsuleer mercenary alliance. Lost a Ragnarok-class Titan YC109 in capsuleer-vs-capsuleer combat — the precedent event that established Titan-class targets as feasible objectives for coordinated capsuleer-alliance operations. Continues to operate as a hire-able mercenary-alliance faction.

nodes.name: Paxton Federation (capsuleer alliance)
nodes.brief: Capsuleer alliance — Amarr-loyalist faction. Imperial-aligned operational support.
nodes.master_summary: Capsuleer alliance — Amarr-loyalist faction. Imperial-aligned operational support; one of the capsuleer alliances providing political validation for the Empire's claim to capsuleer-faction allegiance.

nodes.name: Orvolle
nodes.brief: High-security system in the Bayet constellation, Placid region; directly stargate-connected to the Syndicate region. Site of the first Intaki Syndicate breakout interception (YC128.05.07), where the Syndicate forced a cynosural field in high-sec using an unregistered mobile beacon.
nodes.master_summary: Border high-sec system the Caldari militia flagged as a likely Syndicate evacuation corridor (the Caldari called the surrounding Bayet constellation the 'bait constellation'). The Scope's Alton Haveri anchored the YC128.05.07 broadcast from here.

nodes.name: Alton Haveri
nodes.brief: The Scope field reporter. Anchored the live YC128.05.07 broadcast from Orvolle covering the destruction of the first Intaki Syndicate breakout fleet.

nodes.name: Heika Torigo
nodes.brief: Caldari Navy Executive General. Directed the post-conquest bounty program against Intaki Syndicate vessels and warned loyalist capsuleers (a few days before YC128.05.07) that multiple Syndicate breakout attempts were anticipated over the following two days.

nodes.name: Orvolle Breakout Interception
nodes.brief: First of three anticipated Intaki Syndicate capital breakout attempts. A Syndicate fleet (3× Moros, 2× Nyx, 1× Erebus-class Titan) jumped to an unregistered mobile cynosural beacon in high-sec Orvolle, was flagged CONCORD-suspect, and was destroyed by capsuleer loyalists; capital-grade weapons proved ineffective against sub-capitals in high-sec.
nodes.master_summary: The canon-confirmed realization of one of the escort-intercept windows in evt_syndicate_capital_breakout. Demonstrates the Syndicate inherited / refined the YC123 high-sec cyno-beacon tech (the Tanu II / Junfaa precedent). The Scope covered it live (Alton Haveri, from Orvolle); Caldari intelligence had apparently compromised the Syndicate's plans, and General Heika Torigo's communiqué had pre-warned that two further attempts were expected.

nodes.name: YC124 Caldari–Syndicate Protection Pact
nodes.brief: The Caldari State signed a protection agreement with the Intaki Syndicate in exchange for control of a border constellation (auto-caption: 'DSTC-M4Q'). Reflected the historically sympathetic State–Syndicate relationship; dissolved once the State attributed the YC128 Jita-gate attacks to the Syndicate.

nodes.name: Trial of Silphy en Diabel
nodes.brief: Scheduled for YC128.05.19 — the Tuesday after Fanfest, the trigger for the final week of the Warpath live-event cycle. Silphy's legal representative read a statement asserting her innocence and telling the authorities not to waste their time on a trial — widely read as ominous (Syndicate retaliation anticipated).

nodes.name: Jita Under CONCORD Administration
nodes.brief: Jita placed under direct CONCORD administration. Gallente Federation militia may now operate in the system without triggering a CONCORD response, ending Jita's standing as a faction-warfare consideration. Station ownership and tax revenue remain with Caldari Navy. The change codifies a long-running player-base and CSM push for a faction-neutral Jita, accelerated by the Jita-gate incident and Silphy's arrest.
nodes.master_summary: The structural resolution of the Jita-gate arc. Rather than restoring the status quo, CONCORD removed Jita from the faction-warfare board entirely — Gallente militia can operate there unhindered while Caldari Navy keeps the station revenue. Closes the YC128 high-sec-flashpoint thread (Jita-gate → Warpath → Silphy → here) by changing the jurisdiction of the contested space rather than re-securing it. The trade-hub function is untouched (taxes still flow to Caldari Navy), so the market impact is minimal — the change is jurisdictional, not commercial; the actual trade-geography move to watch is the parallel AIR trade hub being established near Exordium, positioned to compete with Dodixie.

nodes.name: Destruction of the Urinichi 'Forazar'
nodes.brief: A Triglavian-aligned structure in the Urinichi system, Pochven (transcribed on-stream as a 'Forazar' — name possibly garbled), destroyed amid the ongoing fight over Pochven's observation points. Because Pochven structures carry no asset safety, the kill produced a debris field of roughly 1.5 trillion ISK in red ('precursor') loot; a blockade runner that recovered a large share of it was destroyed by Baba Yagas defenders while attempting to dock in known space. Reported by Ashterothi; ISK figures via Drake's analysis.
